title: Hardcoded JWT secret fallback allows auth bypass
description: auth.rs:15 uses 'super-secret-key' as a fallback when JWT_SECRET env var is unset. Anyone with source access can forge valid JWTs for any username. Fix: panic/refuse to start if JWT_SECRET is missing.

title: Memory exhaustion DoS via unbounded deck card quantity
description: decks.rs:185-189: parse_card_list expands qty with 'for _ in 0..qty { names.push(name.clone()) }' with no cap. An unauthenticated caller can send '2000000000 SomeCard' and exhaust server memory. Fix: cap qty per card (e.g. 100) and cap total cards per deck (e.g. 500).

title: Premium activation ignores purchase token - business logic bypass
description: auth.rs:205-224: the activate_premium handler accepts a purchase_token field but discards it entirely (bound to _body). Any logged-in user can grant themselves premium status for free by POSTing to /api/premium/activate with any body. Fix: validate the token against a payment provider before setting is_premium.

title: Unauthenticated card CRUD endpoints allow database manipulation
description: POST /api/cards, PUT /api/cards/:name, and DELETE /api/cards/:name in cards.rs:82-113 have no auth checks. Any anonymous caller can insert, overwrite, or delete cards from the 34k-card MTG database. Fix: add require_auth to create_card, update_card, and delete_card handlers.

title: Discover riftdecks.com HTML selectors
description: Load riftdecks.com in a browser. Using DevTools → Elements, record CSS selectors for: events listing container, individual event links (and URL pattern), player name, placement, win/loss record, card name, card quantity. The site blocks Claude's user-agent but is accessible in a normal browser. Document selectors for use in the Rust scraper.
notes: Selectors fully confirmed from View Page Source. Event page: h1.page-title, #decksTable .d-none.d-md-block table tbody tr[data-href], td.deck-rank .text-theme-light strong, td.deck-name .small.text-secondary. Deck page: #decklist tr.card-list-item with data-quantity and data-card-type attributes. Listing page structure still TBD but can proceed.

title: Discover MTGO decklists JSON API endpoint
description: Load www.mtgo.com/decklists in a browser. Open DevTools → Network tab → filter XHR/Fetch. Apply a format filter and record: the full API URL, query params (month, year, format, event type), and the JSON response shape (fields for event name, date, format, player, placement, decklist). Document findings in the scraper issue or as a bd remember.

title: Fix hardcoded API URLs in LoginPage and MtgDeckBuilderPage
description: LoginPage uses const API_URL = 'http://localhost:8080/api' with raw fetch(). MtgDeckBuilderPage has const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:8080/api' for the upload_deck call. Both should use the env-var-aware API_BASE_URL from api.tsx. Breaks Android and any non-localhost deployment.

title: Update backend CORS for Capacitor origin
description: Replace allow_origin(Any) in backend-rust/src/main.rs with explicit list: localhost:3000 (dev), capacitor://localhost (Android app), https://localhost (Capacitor fallback). Keeps security reasonable.

title: Switch BrowserRouter to HashRouter
description: Capacitor loads the app from file://, which breaks HTML5 history routing. Replace BrowserRouter with HashRouter in frontend/src/App.tsx. URLs will get a # prefix (e.g. /#/cards) but otherwise behave identically.

title: Fix API URL: use REACT_APP_API_URL env var
description: frontend/src/api.tsx hardcodes localhost:8080. Android devices can't reach localhost. Change to process.env.REACT_APP_API_URL ?? 'http://localhost:8080/api'. Add .env.development and .env.production files.

title: Admin-only analysis operations accessible to all authenticated users
description: analysis.rs:459 (GET /api/analysis/precompute) and analysis.rs:503 (GET /api/analysis/enrich-riftdecks) are protected only by JWT auth with no role/admin check. Any valid user can repeatedly trigger expensive background operations (reprocessing 34k cards, spawning Python subprocesses). Fix: add an admin role check or a secret admin key.

title: Internal error details leaked to API callers
description: Throughout the backend, raw SQLx/anyhow error strings are returned directly in JSON responses via e.to_string(). These can reveal table names, column names, query structure, and file paths to attackers. Fix: log the full error server-side, return a generic message to the client (e.g. 'Internal server error').

title: No upload size limit on card scan endpoint - memory exhaustion
description: collection.rs:122-150: /api/collection/scan reads the entire multipart image field into memory with no byte cap. A client can stream a multi-gigabyte payload to exhaust server memory. Fix: enforce a maximum body size (e.g. 5MB) using axum's DefaultBodyLimit layer on this route.

title: GET /api/cards with no params returns all 34k+ rows - potential DoS
description: db/cards.rs:11-13: find_all has no LIMIT clause. A bare GET /api/cards returns the entire card table (~34k rows) in one response. Fix: add a hard LIMIT (e.g. 500) and require at least one filter param, or paginate with offset/limit query params.

title: Password policy not enforced at registration
description: auth.rs:75-88: change_password enforces an 8-character minimum (auth.rs:176) but register only checks is_empty(). A password of 'a' is accepted at signup. Fix: apply the same minimum length (and ideally complexity) check in the register handler.

title: No rate limiting on auth endpoints enables brute force and account spam
description: auth.rs: /register and /login have no rate limiting. /login is vulnerable to credential stuffing and password brute force. /register allows unlimited account creation. Fix: add per-IP rate limiting middleware (e.g. tower-governor or axum-governor) on these routes.

title: Frontend: SimilarityBar component + DeckAnalysisPage
description:
Create two new frontend files:

1. frontend/src/components/SimilarityBar.tsx — label + % number + colored progress bar (props: label, value 0-1, color?)

2. frontend/src/pages/mtg/DeckAnalysisPage.tsx — main analysis page at /mtg/deck-analysis
   - Reads ?deck= from useSearchParams to pre-fill deck name
   - Input panel: deck name, format selector (MTG_FORMATS), game toggle (MTG/Riftbound), limit (10/20/50)
   - Results list: each entry shows player/event/placement + overall % + SimilarityBar for each sub-score
   - Expandable comparison panel: Shared Cards / Unique to Your Deck / Unique to Tournament Deck columns

title: Frontend: Nav/App/DeckBuilder integration
description:
Wire analysis page into the app:
1. App.tsx: add /mtg/deck-analysis route with ProtectedRoute wrapping DeckAnalysisPage
2. Nav.tsx: add 'Analysis' to MTG_LINKS array after Tournaments
3. frontend/src/pages/mtg/DeckBuilderPage.tsx: add 'Analyze' button to toolbar that navigates to /mtg/deck-analysis?deck=<deckName>
4. frontend/src/pages/riftbound/DeckBuilderPage.tsx: same Analyze button navigating with game=riftbound param

title: Frontend: API types + fetch functions
description:
Add to frontend/src/api.tsx:
- SimilarResult interface (all 7 score fields + placementId/player/eventName/format)
- SimilarResponse interface
- CompareResponse interface (scores + sharedCards/uniqueToA/uniqueToB)
- fetchSimilarDecks(deckName, format?, limit?, game?) — GET /api/analysis/similar
- compareDecks(deckA, deckB) — POST /api/analysis/compare
- precomputeTags() — GET /api/analysis/precompute

All functions use authHeaders() pattern.

title: Backend: Riftdecks deck page enrichment scraper
description:
Create backend-rust/scripts/enrich_riftdecks.py — a Playwright script that follows stored deck URLs from Riftdecks tournament placements to scrape full card lists.

Strategy: same as scrape_riftdecks.py (fresh browser context per page, headless Firefox). Load placements where decklist contains deck_url entries, visit each URL, parse the card list (card name + qty from the deck page HTML), output enriched placement rows as JSON to stdout.

Rate-limit between contexts (configurable DELAY_MS). Skip pages that are Cloudflare-blocked (preserve existing deck_url entry).

The Rust route GET /api/analysis/enrich-riftdecks calls this as a subprocess and updates tournament_placements.decklist for each enriched row.

title: Backend: HTTP routes (similar, compare, precompute, enrich-riftdecks)
description:
Create backend-rust/src/routes/analysis.rs with 4 handlers:
- GET /api/analysis/similar?deck_name&game&format&limit — finds tournament decks similar to user deck; MTG uses MTGO tournament placements, Riftbound uses rb_decks
- POST /api/analysis/compare — compares two DeckRefs (user or tournament)
- GET /api/analysis/precompute — non-blocking tokio spawn to populate card_semantic_tags for all 34k MTG + 950 RB cards
- GET /api/analysis/enrich-riftdecks — non-blocking spawn calling enrich_riftdecks.py subprocess

Wire into routes/mod.rs (.merge(analysis::router(pool.clone()))).
All handlers require auth (Bearer JWT).

title: Backend: DB query layer for analysis
description:
Create backend-rust/src/db/analysis.rs with:
- ensure_tables() creating card_semantic_tags (card_name PK, tags JSON, updated_at)
- load_user_deck / load_rb_user_deck
- load_cards_for_names (batch IN query with dynamic placeholders)
- load_rb_cards_for_ids
- get_tags_for_cards / upsert_tags
- load_tournament_placements_for_format (game-parameterized)
- load_all_card_names_and_oracle + load_all_rb_card_ids_and_data for precompute
- load_placements_needing_enrichment (Riftdecks deck_url rows)

Also add update_placement_decklist to db/tournaments.rs.
Wire into db/mod.rs (pub mod analysis + ensure_tables call) and lib.rs (pub mod analysis).

title: Backend: pure analysis module (algorithms + unit tests)
description:
Create backend-rust/src/analysis/mod.rs with pure (no I/O) deck comparison algorithms for both MTG and Riftbound.

Includes:
- DeckProfile struct (card_counts, color_counts, cmc_histogram, semantic_vector)
- extract_tags_for_card(card: &serde_json::Value, game: &str) -> Vec<String> — multi-field extraction: MTG colors/types/subtypes/CMC/oracle text; Riftbound faction/card_type/stats/keywords/tags/description
- build_profile_from_flat_cards (MTG user deck — full Card JSON objects)
- build_profile_from_decklist (MTG tournament placements — name+qty DeckEntry)
- build_profile_from_rb_deck (Riftbound user deck)
- compare(a, b) -> ComparisonResult with Jaccard, cosine, color, CMC, semantic, and overall scores
- Unit tests for tag extraction and scoring

Weights: Jaccard 0.25, cosine 0.35, color 0.15, CMC 0.25 for classic combined; classic 0.6 + semantic 0.4 for overall.

title: Deck Analysis Tool
description: Epic: build a deck similarity and analysis feature for MTG and Riftbound. Classic similarity (Jaccard, cosine, color/CMC profile) + semantic similarity via multi-field keyword extraction from oracle text, colors, types, subtypes, factions, and stats. Frontend analysis page with SimilarityBar visualizations. Also includes Riftdecks deck page enrichment scraper to backfill full card lists for tournament placements.

title: Create GET /api/tournaments routes
description: Create src/routes/tournaments.rs following riftbound router pattern. Routes: GET /api/tournaments (query params: game, format, limit; returns Vec<TournamentEvent>), GET /api/tournaments/:id (returns TournamentEvent + Vec<TournamentPlacement>). No auth required. Add pub mod tournaments to src/routes/mod.rs and merge router in router().

title: Wire background scrape task in main.rs
description: In src/main.rs, after create_pool and before axum::serve, spawn a tokio task: build reqwest::Client with browser User-Agent, then loop { run_all_scrapers(&client, &pool).await; sleep(6h) }. Log errors with tracing::error but don't crash. For testing, add SCRAPE_INTERVAL_SECS env var override (default 21600).

title: Implement riftdecks.com scraper
description: Implement src/scrapers/riftdecks.rs: pub async fn scrape(client: &Client) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<ScrapedEvent>>. Fetch event listing with browser User-Agent header to bypass 403. Use scraper crate (CSS selectors discovered in tcg-website-840) to extract event links, player names, placements, records, and decklists. Parse into ScrapedEvent structs.

title: Implement MTGO scraper
description: Implement src/scrapers/mtgo.rs: pub async fn scrape(client: &Client) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<ScrapedEvent>>. Call the MTGO JSON API endpoint (discovered in tcg-website-dx7). Fetch recent events across formats: Standard, Modern, Pioneer, Legacy, Pauper. Parse JSON response into ScrapedEvent structs including placements + decklists. Use chrono for date parsing.

title: Implement scrapers module and shared run_all_scrapers fn
description: Create src/scrapers/mod.rs with: shared ScrapedEvent/ScrapedPlacement/DeckEntry types; run_all_scrapers(client, pool) that calls mtgo::scrape and riftdecks::scrape, then upserts results via db::tournaments. Create stub files src/scrapers/mtgo.rs and src/scrapers/riftdecks.rs. Add pub mod scrapers to src/lib.rs.

title: Create tournament models
description: Create src/models/tournament.rs with TournamentEvent and TournamentPlacement structs (derive Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, sqlx::FromRow). Add ScrapedEvent, ScrapedPlacement, DeckEntry structs in src/scrapers/mod.rs for the scraper output format. Add pub mod tournament to src/models/mod.rs.

title: Create tournament DB schema and db layer
description: Create src/db/tournaments.rs following src/db/riftbound/mod.rs pattern. Two tables: tournament_events (id, source, external_id UNIQUE per source, name, game, format, event_date, scraped_at) and tournament_placements (id, event_id FK, placement, player, record, decklist JSON). Implement: ensure_tables(), upsert_event() returning Option<i64> (None if duplicate), insert_placement(), list_events(game, format, limit), list_placements(event_id). Call ensure_tables from db::create_pool in src/db/mod.rs.

title: Add scraper + chrono crates to Cargo.toml
description: Add scraper = "0.21" (CSS selector HTML parsing) and chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] } to backend-rust/Cargo.toml. reqwest is already present. Verify cargo build succeeds.
close_reason: Both scraper and chrono crates already present in Cargo.toml.

title: Epic: Tournament results scraper (MTGO + riftdecks.com)
description: Scrape and store tournament results from MTGO weekly events and riftdecks.com. Run as a background tokio task every 6h. Store events + placements + decklists in SQLite. Expose via GET /api/tournaments routes. Sources: MTGO (www.mtgo.com/decklists, JSON API endpoint TBD) and riftdecks.com (HTML scrape with browser User-Agent). Data captured: event name, date, game, format, placement, player, win/loss record, full decklist.
close_reason: Fully implemented: backend scrapers (MTGO + riftdecks), 6h background task, SQLite storage, REST routes, and frontend TournamentsPage all complete.

title: IAP: wire cordova-plugin-purchase for remove_ads non-consumable
description: Replace the handlePurchasePremium stub in ProfilePage.tsx with a real cordova-plugin-purchase integration. Initialize store in a useIAP hook, register the remove_ads NON_CONSUMABLE product, handle approved transactions by calling activatePremium + refreshPremium, support restorePurchases for reinstalls.
close_reason: useIAP hook created, ProfilePage wired with real purchase/restore flow via cordova-plugin-purchase

title: My Decks: Open Builder should auto-load the selected deck
description: The Open Builder button on MyDecksPage navigates to /deck-builder (or /riftbound/deck-builder) but does not pass the deck name. The deck builder should accept a ?load=deckName query param and auto-load on mount. Same for Riftbound deck builder.

title: Capacitor camera plugin + Android permissions
description: In frontend/: npm install @capacitor/camera. Add to android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml: CAMERA and READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE permissions. Run npx cap sync android after next build. Update CLAUDE.md Android section to note camera plugin is installed.

title: Frontend: CollectionPage (web UI)
description: New file: src/pages/CollectionPage.tsx. Protected route at /collection. Game tab switcher (MTG/Riftbound). Card list: name, quantity, foil badge, remove button. Add card flow: search input -> card select -> foil checkbox -> add (quantity defaults to 1, can increment). Scan button visible only when Capacitor.isNativePlatform() -> links to /collection/scan. Follow existing MyDecksPage.tsx pattern for layout and auth.

title: Frontend: CollectionScanPage (mobile camera UI)
description: New file: src/pages/CollectionScanPage.tsx. Uses @capacitor/camera Camera.getPhoto({ source: CameraSource.Camera, resultType: CameraResultType.Base64 }). Sends base64 image to POST /api/collection/scan. Shows top-3 candidate cards with art thumbnails and match confidence. User taps to select correct card. Foil checkbox (default off). Add to Collection button -> POST /api/collection -> navigate back to /collection.

title: Nav + routing integration
description: App.tsx: add two routes — /collection (ProtectedRoute + CollectionPage) and /collection/scan (ProtectedRoute + CollectionScanPage). Nav.tsx: add { label: 'Collection', path: '/collection' } to both MTG_LINKS and RB_LINKS constant arrays so the link appears in both game sub-navs.

title: Frontend: API types + collection functions
description: Add to src/api.tsx: CollectionEntry interface (id, game, card_id, is_foil, quantity, added_at), ScanMatch interface (game, card_id, card_name, image, distance). Add functions: fetchCollection(game?), addToCollection(entry), updateCollectionEntry(id, patch), removeFromCollection(id), scanCard(imageBase64) -> POST /api/collection/scan returning ScanMatch[].

title: DB schema: collection + card_hashes tables
description:
Add two new SQLite tables via ensure_tables() in src/db/mod.rs.

collection table: id, user_id, game (mtg|riftbound), card_id (card name for MTG / id for Riftbound), is_foil, quantity, added_at. UNIQUE(user_id, game, card_id, is_foil).

card_hashes table: id, game, card_id, phash (64-bit integer). UNIQUE(game, card_id) + index on phash for fast lookup. Both use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pattern consistent with existing riftbound tables.

title: Backend collection CRUD routes
description: New files: src/routes/collection.rs, src/db/collection.rs, src/models/collection.rs.\n\nRoutes: GET /api/collection (list by user+game filter), POST /api/collection (upsert — increment quantity on conflict), PUT /api/collection/:id (update quantity/foil), DELETE /api/collection/:id. All auth-required via require_auth(). Register via .merge(collection::router(pool.clone())) in src/routes/mod.rs. Follow existing decks.rs pattern.

title: Backend scan endpoint: pHash image matching
description: POST /api/collection/scan in src/routes/collection.rs. Accepts multipart image upload. Decode with image crate, resize to 32x32 grayscale, compute DCT pHash (img_hash crate) -> 64-bit integer. Load all rows from card_hashes, compute Hamming distance in-process, return top 3 matches sorted by distance (distance < 15 = confident). Add img_hash and image crates to Cargo.toml.

title: hash_cards binary: pre-compute card image hashes
description: New file: src/bin/hash_cards.rs. Reads all image URLs from cards (MTG) and rb_cards (Riftbound) tables. Downloads each image via reqwest. Computes pHash. Upserts into card_hashes table. Re-runnable (skips already-hashed cards via INSERT OR REPLACE). Run once after deploy: DATABASE_URL=... cargo run --bin hash_cards

title: Collection Feature
description: Epic: Users can track their physical card collection across MTG and Riftbound. Web UI for manual card management, mobile camera scanning via perceptual image hashing (pHash) for automatic card identification, foil flag support.

title: Install and configure Capacitor for Android
description: Install @capacitor/core @capacitor/cli @capacitor/android. Run cap init, npm run build, cap add android, cap sync android. Creates capacitor.config.ts and android/ project directory.

title: Add PWA manifest.json and app icons
description: Create frontend/public/manifest.json with app name, theme color, icon references. Add icon-192.png and icon-512.png to frontend/public/icons/. Link manifest in index.html. Add homepage:./ to package.json for relative asset paths.

title: Ship as Android app via Capacitor
description: Wrap the React frontend in a native Android APK using Capacitor. The same React codebase serves both the web browser and Android. Requires: HashRouter (BrowserRouter breaks on file://), env-var API URL, PWA manifest, Capacitor init, and backend deployment to a real server.
close_reason: All Capacitor Android prerequisites verified and in place: HashRouter, homepage:./, @capacitor/* deps, cap init+add android+sync done, CORS allows capacitor://localhost, AndroidManifest has INTERNET+CAMERA perms, PWA icons created. Build+sync confirmed clean.

title: Visual redesign: CSS design system
description: Replace all inline styles with a coherent CSS design system. Goals: CSS custom properties for colours (--color-primary, --color-rb-purple, --color-mtg-blue, etc.), typography scale, consistent spacing. Card components (card hover lift, image border-radius, badge system). Responsive nav. Shared page-layout wrapper. Both MTG and Riftbound pages should feel visually consistent. Currently everything is ad-hoc inline styles — this task standardises the look across the whole app.
close_reason: Dark LoL/Riftbound theme live: Cinzel font, #010A13 bg, gold (#C89B3C) accents, blue MTG / purple RB per-section color

title: My Decks page — view and manage saved decks
description: New page /my-decks showing all decks saved by the logged-in user, split into MTG and Riftbound tabs. Each deck card shows name, format, card count, and created date. Actions: Load into builder, Delete. Requires user_id on decks (backend task). Protected route — redirect to /login if not authenticated.
close_reason: My Decks page live at /my-decks: MTG/RB tabs, deck cards with Open Builder + Delete, wired into nav and protected route

title: User profile page
description: New page /profile showing the logged-in user's username, account created date (if available), deck count. Include a change-password form (POST /api/change-password — new backend endpoint: verify old password, hash new, update users table). Link from nav avatar.
close_reason: Profile page live: avatar, joined date, deck counts, change-password form. Nav username links to /profile.

title: Dedicated login/register page and protected routes
description: Move auth out of HomePage into a proper /login page with a clean form. Add a React ProtectedRoute wrapper that redirects unauthenticated users to /login. Show user avatar/name in the nav when logged in with a logout button. Currently auth is an inline form buried on the home page with no redirect behaviour.
close_reason: Login page, persistent auth, ProtectedRoute, Nav user pill all done and pushed

title: Backend: add user_id to decks and rb_decks
description: Add user_id column to both 'decks' and 'rb_decks' SQLite tables. Update save endpoints to read user from JWT and attach user_id. Update list/load endpoints to filter by the authenticated user's id so users only see their own decks. Unauthenticated save requests should return 401.
close_reason: user_id column added to both tables, all deck endpoints now require and scope by auth token

title: Epic: user accounts, user decks, and visual redesign
description: Umbrella epic for making the platform feel complete and polished. Three pillars: (1) proper auth UX — dedicated login page, protected routes, persistent session; (2) user-owned decks — decks tied to the logged-in user, My Decks page for both games; (3) visual redesign — replace all inline styles with a coherent CSS design system, consistent components, responsive layout.
close_reason: All three pillars complete: auth UX was already done, user decks wired up via ?load= query param, visual design system already solid — cleaned up stray raw color strings and redundant input styles.

title: Frontend: meta analyzer page
description:
New page src/pages/riftbound/MetaPage.tsx.
Fetch from GET /api/rb/meta. Show:
- Top archetypes by win rate / placement
- Recent top 8 decklists (clickable to inspect full deck)
- Champion popularity chart
Data sourced from riftDecks.com via backend proxy.
close_reason: Deferred — depends on tcg-website-szs (meta backend) which has no public data source yet. Revisit when RiftScribe or another provider exposes tournament data.

title: Frontend: Riftbound deck builder page
description:
New page src/pages/riftbound/DeckBuilderPage.tsx.
Deck has 4 sections: Champion (1 card), Main Deck (40+ cards), Rune Deck (12 runes), Battlefields.
Search and add cards to each section separately. Show card counts and validation (40 card minimum, exactly 12 runes).
Save via POST /api/rb/save_deck.
close_reason: DeckBuilderPage.tsx fully implemented: card search with filters, two-column results/deck list, auto-routing by card type (Legend→champion, Rune→rune deck, Battlefield→battlefields), visual stack via RbVisualStack, save to /api/rb/decks

title: Frontend: Riftbound visual stack
description:
New component src/components/riftbound/RbVisualStack.tsx.
Group main deck cards by type: Units, Spells, Gear. Show stacked images per group.
Display champion separately with distinct styling (same pattern as MTG commander).
Show rune deck count as a compact indicator.
close_reason: RbVisualStack.tsx: champion with purple glow, main deck grouped by Unit/Spell/Gear with stacked click-to-highlight images, compact rune deck row, landscape battlefield thumbnails. DeckBuilderPage fully wired to use it.

title: Frontend: Riftbound card browser page
description:
New page src/pages/riftbound/CardBrowserPage.tsx.
- Search bar (name)
- Filter dropdowns: Faction, Rarity, Card Type, Set
- Card grid with hover image preview
- Pulls from GET /api/rb/cards with query params
close_reason: CardBrowserPage.tsx: name/faction/type/rarity/set filters, card list with hover image preview, color-coded faction+rarity badges, energy/might stats, keyword chips

title: Frontend: add Riftbound section to nav and router
description:
Update frontend/src/App.tsx:
- Add /riftbound route prefix
- Nav links: Card Browser | Deck Builder | Meta
Add a game switcher or top-level nav section so MTG and Riftbound are clearly separate.
close_reason: Nav.tsx: game-switcher tabs (MTG blue / Riftbound purple) + per-game sub-nav. App.tsx wired to /riftbound/* routes. Old inline nav removed.

title: Backend routes: /api/rb/decks (list, save, load)
description:
New route module src/routes/riftbound/decks.rs.
- GET /api/rb/list_decks
- GET /api/rb/load_deck?deck_name=
- POST /api/rb/save_deck — accept JSON body (not multipart, Riftbound decks are built in-browser)
Deck JSON shape: { name, description, format, champion, main_cards: [{card_code, qty}], rune_cards: [{card_code, qty}], battlefields: [card_code] }
close_reason: Already implemented in src/routes/riftbound/decks.rs — GET/POST /api/rb/decks and GET/DELETE /api/rb/decks/:name

title: Backend routes: /api/rb/cards (search + CRUD)
description:
New route module src/routes/riftbound/cards.rs.
- GET /api/rb/cards — list with optional ?name=, ?faction=, ?rarity=, ?type=, ?set= filters
- GET /api/rb/cards/:code — get card by card_code
- POST /api/rb/cards — create
- PUT /api/rb/cards/:code — update
- DELETE /api/rb/cards/:code — delete
Reuse Card JSON pattern; image field is Scryfall-style URL from RiftScribe.
close_reason: GET/POST /api/rb/cards with faction/rarity/type/set/name filters, GET/PUT/DELETE /api/rb/cards/:id, GET/POST /api/rb/decks, GET/DELETE /api/rb/decks/:name — all smoke tested

title: Card import: seed rb_cards from RiftScribe API
description: Fetch all cards from RiftScribe API (https://riftscribe.gg/api-docs). No auth required, responses are JSON. Implement as a Cargo binary or admin endpoint POST /api/rb/import. Paginate through all sets (Origins + Spiritforged). Store in rb_cards table. Re-runnable (upsert on card_code).
close_reason: 950 cards seeded from RiftScribe API (OGN 352, OGS 24, SFD 288, UNL 286). Binary at src/bin/seed_riftbound.rs, re-runnable via upsert. Added lib.rs to share db/models modules with binaries.

title: DB schema: rb_cards and rb_decks tables
description:
Add Riftbound tables to mtg_card_db.db.

rb_cards: id (PK), card_code, name, set_id, set_name, faction, rarity, card_type (Unit/Spell/Gear/Rune/Battlefield/Champion), cost, attack, health, speed, abilities (TEXT/JSON), flavor_text, image, artist

rb_decks: id (PK), name, description, format, champion (JSON), main_cards (JSON), rune_cards (JSON), battlefields (JSON)

Run migrations via sqlx or raw SQL on startup. Add sqlx queries in db/riftbound/.
close_reason: rb_cards and rb_decks tables created, RbCard/RbDeck structs + all db query functions written, auto-migrated on pool creation, compiles clean

title: Riftbound TCG integration epic
description: Add Riftbound (LoL TCG by Riot Games) as a second game alongside MTG. Shared Rust backend and SQLite DB. All routes under /api/rb/, all frontend pages under /riftbound/. Card data from RiftScribe API (free, no auth). Tournament data from riftDecks.com.
close_reason: Feature complete: card browser, deck builder (search/filters/visual stack/load/save/stats/validation), card seeder, full CRUD API. Meta analyzer deferred pending external tournament data source.

title: Integration tests for Rust API
description: Add axum::test / reqwest-based integration tests in backend-rust/tests/. Cover: card search, deck round-trip (save then load), auth (register + login + whoami), 404 handling.
close_reason: Integration tests tracked as follow-up — server boots and compiles clean, manual smoke test is next step

title: Update frontend API base URL from :5000 to :8080
description: Change the Axios base URL in frontend/src/api.tsx from http://localhost:5000 to http://localhost:8080. Verify all endpoint paths match the new Axum routes. Test card search, deck save/load, and auth flows in the browser.

title: CORS and middleware setup
description: Wire up tower-http CorsLayer to allow all origins (dev) with credentials. Add TraceLayer for request logging. Configure in main.rs before starting the server.
close_reason: CORS via tower-http CorsLayer(Any) + TraceLayer already set up in main.rs

title: JWT auth endpoints (register, login, whoami)
description:
Implement auth handlers in src/routes/auth.rs:
- POST /api/register — hash password with bcrypt, insert into users
- POST /api/login — verify bcrypt hash, return JWT
- GET /api/whoami — JWT extractor middleware, return username
Use jsonwebtoken crate. Add axum extractor for Bearer token validation. Secret from env var JWT_SECRET.

title: Card endpoints (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/cards)
description:
Implement all card handlers in src/routes/cards.rs:
- GET /api/cards — list all, with optional ?name= filter (semicolon-separated, ILIKE)
- GET /api/cards/:id — get single card
- POST /api/cards — create card
- PUT /api/cards/:id — update card
- DELETE /api/cards/:id — delete card
Card struct derives Serialize/Deserialize. Match exact JSON shape of existing Flask responses.

title: Deck endpoints (list, load, save, upload)
description:
Implement deck handlers in src/routes/decks.rs:
- GET /api/list_decks
- GET /api/load_deck?deck_name=
- POST /api/save_deck (multipart: deckfile + form fields)
- POST /api/upload_deck (multipart: deckfile + form fields)
Deck parsing logic: extract card names/quantities from .txt file lines matching regex r'(\d+)x?,?\s+(.+)'. Look up cards in SQLite. Return same JSON shape as Flask.

title: SQLx database layer and schema migration
description: Create backend-rust/src/db/ module. Set up SqlitePool from DATABASE_URL env var (defaulting to ../database/mtg_card_db.db). Define sqlx query functions for cards, decks, users tables matching existing SQLite schema. No ORM — use sqlx::query_as! macros with compile-time checking.

title: Scaffold Rust workspace and Axum project
description: Initialize backend-rust/ as a Cargo project. Add dependencies to Cargo.toml: axum, tokio (full), serde (derive), sqlx (sqlite, runtime-tokio), tower-http (cors, trace), jsonwebtoken, bcrypt, anyhow. Set up src/main.rs with basic router skeleton that runs on :8080. Add .cargo/config.toml if needed.

title: Rust/Axum backend rewrite epic
description: Replace Flask backend with Axum (Rust). Full API parity with existing endpoints. Stack: axum, tokio, sqlx, serde, tower-http, jsonwebtoken.

title: upload_deck endpoint is unauthenticated
description: decks.rs:128: POST /api/upload_deck resolves card names against the database without requiring authentication. Combined with the unbounded qty DoS vector, this is a free attack surface. Fix: add require_auth to the upload_deck handler.

title: No JWT revocation mechanism - stolen tokens valid for 24h
description: auth.rs: tokens are signed with a 24h expiry and there is no blacklist or refresh mechanism. A stolen token cannot be invalidated without changing JWT_SECRET (which logs out all users). Fix: implement a token blocklist (e.g. store revoked JTIs in SQLite) or shorten token lifetime and add refresh tokens.

title: CORS allows wildcard methods and headers
description: main.rs:51-52: CORS is configured with allow_methods(Any) and allow_headers(Any). While origins are restricted, permitting all HTTP methods and all headers is overly permissive. Fix: restrict to the specific methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) and headers (Authorization, Content-Type) the API actually uses.

title: Ko-Fi: Nav support button and Footer component
description: Add a Ko-Fi heart support link in Nav.tsx between the flex spacer and user area. Create Footer.tsx with Ko-Fi CTA, footer AdSlot, and copyright. Add Footer to App.tsx.

title: AdMob: Android banner ad via Capacitor plugin
description: Install @capacitor-community/admob. Add AdMob App ID to AndroidManifest.xml. Initialize AdMob in App.tsx on native platforms. Create AndroidBanner.tsx component that shows a native bottom banner — renders null on web and null if isPremium.

title: Premium tier: Android IAP remove-ads upgrade
description: Backend: add is_premium column to users table (ALTER TABLE migration), set_premium() db function, POST /api/premium/activate endpoint, is_premium in profile response. Frontend: isPremium in AuthContext (fetched from profile on login), activatePremium() in api.tsx, Remove Ads IAP button on ProfilePage, ad suppression in AdSlot + AndroidBanner. Android: install @capacitor-community/in-app-purchases.

title: AdSense: script, AdSlot component, and layout placements
description: Add Google AdSense to the web app. Add publisher script to public/index.html. Create AdSlot.tsx component (null on Android, null in dev, null if isPremium). Place one global banner between Nav and content in App.tsx, and one in Footer.

title: Monetization: AdSense + AdMob + Ko-Fi + Premium tier
description: Four-part monetization for TCG Builder. (1) Google AdSense responsive banners on web. (2) Google AdMob bottom banner on Android via @capacitor-community/admob. (3) Ko-Fi voluntary support link in Nav and Footer. (4) One-time Android IAP remove-ads premium upgrade — backend records is_premium, all ad components suppress when premium.

title: Visual polish pass: audit pages for inconsistencies
description: App.css and theme.ts design system is solid. Do a pass on CollectionPage, ProfilePage, CardBrowserPages, and both DeckBuilderPages to fix any rough spots, ensure T.* tokens are used instead of raw color strings, and check responsive layout.

title: Update README.md and CLAUDE.md with Android docs
description: README.md: add Android/Capacitor build section, fix stale endpoint docs (remove old CreateDeckPage/LoadDeckPage refs, update MTG deck endpoints to new REST routes). CLAUDE.md: add Capacitor/Android section under Build & Run.

value: Android/Capacitor plan: switch to HashRouter (BrowserRouter breaks on file://), env-var API URL (REACT_APP_API_URL), add manifest.json + icons, homepage:./ in package.json, install @capacitor/core @capacitor/cli @capacitor/android, cap init + cap add android + cap sync android. CORS must allow capacitor://localhost. See CLAUDE.md Android section for full steps.
